zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Beach於大約1588年出生在英國米德萊可霍爾波恩聖 安德魯時，她的父親Robert Beach34歲，母親Grace Colley23歲。她於1611年與James Rawlins or Rollins Sr結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1679年2月23日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆聶文頓享年92歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Beach于大约1588年出生在英国米德萊可霍爾波恩聖 安德魯时，她的父亲Robert Beach34岁，母亲Grace Colley23岁。她于1611年与James Rawlins or Rollins Sr结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1679年2月23日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆聶文頓享年92岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beach
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English beche ‘beech-tree’ (Old English bēce) or Middle English beche ‘stream’ (Old English bæce). Middle English beche may be from Old English bēce ‘beech’ or bæce ‘stream’, and without further evidence it is impossible to distinguish these in Middle English.
Americanized form of Alsatian and German Bitsch: habitational name from Bitche (German Bitsch) in Alsace and Lorraine, at the foot of the northern slope of the Vosges.
Americanized form of German (mainly Hesse) Bitsch: unexplained.

variants.name: Beech
meanings: Americanized form of German or Alsatian Bitsch (see Beach ) or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Beecher
meanings: English (Kent): from Middle English becher, denoting one who lived by a beech-tree, from an agent derivative of Middle English beche ‘beech-tree’ (see Beach ), the -er suffix having a locative sense. This English name is also common in Ireland, particularly in Cork.
Americanized form of German Bücher (cognate with 1 above) (see Bucher ).

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: Massachusetts Colony Gains Control of New Hampshire
historicEvents.description: In 1641, Massachusetts gained control of New Hampshire until 1679 when New Hampshire became a separate royal colony.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Beach was born about 1588, in St Andrew, Holborn, Middlesex, England, her father, Robert Beach, was 34 and her mother, Grace Colley, was 23. She married James Rawlins or Rollins Sr in 1611. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 23 February 1679, in Newington, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, at the age of 92.

ko.Newington, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Br: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 뉴잉턴
ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Beach(이)가 대략 1588년 영국 미들레세스 홀본 스트 앤드루에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Beach(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Grace Colley(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1611년 James Rawlins or Rollins Sr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1679년 2월 23일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 뉴잉턴에서 92세의 나이로 사망했다.

Она умерла 23 февраля 1679 г. в местности нюингтон, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 92 лет.
